steps.title: Use the square area formula
steps.content: The area of a square is side length times side length. With side length $86$ centimeters, the area expression must be $86\cdot 86$.

steps.title: Recall the area formula for a square
steps.content: A square with side length s has area = s × s = s².

steps.title: Substitute the given side length
steps.content: The side length is 86 cm, so: Area = 86 × 86 cm²

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $2\cdot 86$: this uses only two side lengths, not side times side. Eliminated.
  • $2\cdot 86\cdot 86$: this doubles the square's area. Eliminated.
  • $4\cdot 86$: this is the perimeter, not the area. Eliminated.
  • $86\cdot 86$: side times side, which is the correct area formula. That is the correct answer.
